conditions.name: Healthy
remedies: Maintain a gentle cleansing routine twice daily
Use broad-spectrum SPF 30+ daily
Stay hydrated and eat a balanced diet

conditions.name: Acne
remedies: Use non-comedogenic, oil-free cleanser twice daily
Apply benzoyl peroxide or salicylic acid as directed
Avoid picking; use non-comedogenic SPF 30+
Reduce high-glycaemic foods; consider dairy reduction
Consult a dermatologist for prescription options if persistent

conditions.name: Eczema
remedies: Apply fragrance-free emollient within 3 minutes of bathing
Use gentle, soap-free cleansers; avoid hot water
Wear cotton clothing; avoid wool and synthetic irritants
Manage stress; consider antihistamines for itch (per doctor)
See a dermatologist for topical steroids or calcineurin inhibitors if needed

conditions.name: Psoriasis
remedies: Apply emollients and moisturisers regularly
Use topical treatments as prescribed (e.g. corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues)
Short, lukewarm showers; avoid harsh soaps
Consider phototherapy or systemic treatment under dermatologist care
Manage triggers: stress, infections, alcohol

conditions.name: Rosacea
remedies: Use gentle, fragrance-free skincare; avoid alcohol-based products
Avoid triggers: spicy food, alcohol, extreme temperatures, sun
Wear broad-spectrum SPF 30+ daily
Consider prescription topical or oral treatments (dermatologist)
Green-tinted makeup can help mask redness if desired

conditions.name: Pigmentation
remedies: Use broad-spectrum SPF 30+ daily; reapply every 2 hours in sun
Consider vitamin C serum or niacinamide (patch test first)
Avoid picking or aggressive scrubbing
Chemical exfoliants (e.g. AHA/BHA) as per product instructions
See a dermatologist for prescription options (e.g. hydroquinone, retinoids)

conditions.name: Dermatitis
remedies: Identify and avoid the irritant or allergen
Use mild soap-free cleansers; apply emollients frequently
Cool compresses for acute flare; avoid scratching
Over-the-counter hydrocortisone (short-term) if appropriate; see doctor if severe

conditions.name: Hives
remedies: Avoid known triggers (foods, medications, heat, stress)
Antihistamines (non-drowsy or at night) as advised by pharmacist/doctor
Cool compresses and loose clothing
If swelling of face/throat or difficulty breathing, seek emergency care

conditions.name: Vitiligo
remedies: Use SPF 30+ on affected and surrounding skin
Camouflage makeup or self-tanning products if desired
Discuss treatment options with a dermatologist (e.g. topical steroids, phototherapy, depigmentation)

conditions.name: Melasma
remedies: Strict sun protection: SPF 50+, hats, shade
Topical treatments per dermatologist (e.g. hydroquinone, retinoids, azelaic acid)
Avoid hormonal triggers if possible; gentle skincare

conditions.name: Seborrheic dermatitis
remedies: Use antifungal or zinc pyrithione shampoo for scalp/face
Apply gentle moisturiser; avoid heavy oils on affected areas
Reduce stress; limit alcohol
See doctor for prescription options if over-the-counter fails

conditions.name: Dry skin
remedies: Use fragrance-free emollients and creams (not just lotions)
Short, lukewarm showers; avoid harsh soaps
Humidifier in dry environments; drink adequate water
Apply moisturiser immediately after bathing

conditions.name: Ringworm
remedies: Apply over-the-counter antifungal cream (e.g. clotrimazole) as directed
Keep area clean and dry; avoid sharing towels
If no improvement in 2 weeks or scalp/nails affected, see a doctor

conditions.name: Impetigo
remedies: Keep sores clean; cover with gauze to reduce spread
Antibiotic cream or oral antibiotics as prescribed by a doctor
Wash hands frequently; avoid touching and sharing towels

conditions.name: Cold sores
remedies: Apply antiviral cream at first sign (e.g. tingling); oral antivirals if prescribed
Avoid touching; wash hands; no sharing utensils or lip products
Use SPF on lips to reduce recurrence
See doctor if frequent, severe, or near eyes

conditions.name: Warts
remedies: Salicylic acid plasters or solution as directed
Do not pick or shave over warts; cover to avoid spread
Cryotherapy or other treatments by doctor if persistent

conditions.name: Boils
remedies: Warm compresses several times a day
Do not squeeze; keep area clean and covered
See doctor if large, on face, or fever; may need incision and drainage or antibiotics

conditions.name: Keloid
remedies: Silicone gel or sheets may help early scars
Corticosteroid injections or laser (dermatologist)
Avoid unnecessary trauma or piercings in prone areas

conditions.name: Lichen planus
remedies: Topical corticosteroids as prescribed
Antihistamines for itch; avoid scratching
Dermatologist for diagnosis and long-term management

conditions.name: Tinea versicolor
remedies: Antifungal shampoo (selenium sulfide or ketoconazole) on skin as directed
Apply to affected areas; leave on then rinse
Recurrence common; may need periodic treatment

conditions.name: Actinic keratosis
remedies: Use broad-spectrum SPF 50+ daily; avoid prolonged sun exposure
Cryotherapy or topical treatments (e.g. 5-fluorouracil, imiquimod) as prescribed
Have a dermatologist review; can be precancerous

conditions.name: Basal cell carcinoma
remedies: Requires medical treatment: surgery, Mohs surgery, or topical therapy as per dermatologist
Use SPF and sun protection to prevent new lesions
Do not delay; see a dermatologist for diagnosis and treatment

conditions.name: Benign keratosis
remedies: Usually no treatment needed unless changing or symptomatic
Gentle cleansing and moisturising
Cryotherapy or curettage by dermatologist if removal desired

conditions.name: Dermatofibroma
remedies: Benign lesion; no action needed unless it changes or bothers you
Use SPF; avoid trauma to the area
Surgical removal only if requested or for diagnosis

conditions.name: Melanoma
remedies: Urgent: see a dermatologist within 1–2 days for evaluation
Do not delay; early detection is critical
Surgery and staging as per oncology/dermatology team

conditions.name: Melanocytic nevus
remedies: Monitor for changes (asymmetry, border, colour, diameter, evolving)
Use SPF when outdoors; avoid excessive sun
See a dermatologist if changing, bleeding, or concerning

conditions.name: Vascular lesion
remedies: Usually benign; no treatment required unless bleeding or growing quickly
Laser or electrocautery by dermatologist if cosmetically desired
See a doctor if it bleeds, grows rapidly, or is painful
